title: coryphantha sneedii
intro: Coryphantha sneedii, also known as the Sneed's pincushion cactus, is a species of cactus in the family Cactaceae. This plant is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, and its stems are covered with fine, yellow or white spines. Coryphantha sneedii has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin infections. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and salves, and is said to have health benefits including reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing.
